title: caltha biflora subsp. howellii
intro: Caltha biflora subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's marsh-marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Caltha biflora subsp. howellii include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested in the spring, dried, and then made into a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
